item.title: Warhammer 40k: Darktide Beyond the Hive Update Brings Expeditions and More Next Week
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Fatshark has announced the release date for Warhammer 40k: Darktide's upcoming Beyond the Hive update, which the studio describes as the game's biggest content injection yet.

In a new trailer, Fatshark confirms that Beyond the Hive will arrive on March 17th, which is just a few days away at time of writing. It's set to dwarf previous updates like last year's Nightmares and Visions and 2024's Unlocked and Loaded.

The big new feature in Beyond the Hive is the Expeditions mode, which is essentially Darktide's take on the currently-popular extraction shooter genre; you'll leave the Hive in order to spend time on the surface of Atoma Prime, scouring the planet for Tech-Remnants for as long as you can before the pollution overwhelms you.

Unlike in other missions, Expeditions won't have set paths, so you'll have to work out where to go amongst your squad. Fatshark says Sites of Interest, which you'll find as you explore Expeditions, can vary significantly; sometimes they veer more towards combat, while at other times, they're "quite puzzle-like".

Exploring Expeditions will also periodically see you entering Sanctuaries, where you'll meet friendlies (well, as close as you're going to get in the Warhammer 40k universe, anyway), heal up, and resupply.

Fatshark seems pretty proud of Expeditions; there's a whole dev blog you can read right here that delves further into what you can expect from the mode. It's not the only thing that's being added as part of Beyond the Hive, though.

Other new stuff includes an expanded arsenal that contains both new gear and "supercharged versions of existing [equipment]", as well as a new Ogryn Pack Master enemy that's "uncharacteristically quick for an Ogryn", as Fatshark says.

Warhammer 40k: Darktide is available right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. The Beyond the Hive update launches on all platforms on March 17th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
